goal: Define task-to-Plan alignment as a nonblocking lifecycle contract.
description: Scope: define the user-visible alignment contract. Context: workspace detection alone cannot identify intent drift, while tool interception blocks recovery. Target: structural activation, latest-intent authority, nonterminal correction, terminal history preservation, host-correct context, privacy, and managed delivery. Design Considerations: distinguish deterministic detection from semantic judgment. Design Value: keep execution aligned without turning workflow guidance into permissions. Constraints & Risks: host prompt protocols differ.

acceptance_criteria.text: Requirements define scope, non-goals, drift correction, and final acceptance.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Requirements.md records the current delivery contract.

goal: Ground lifecycle alignment in repository structure and current host protocols.
description: Scope: collect repository and primary protocol evidence. Context: structural detection and semantic task matching have different reliability boundaries. Target: map current modules and host context capabilities to a safe cross-host design. Design Considerations: separate verified protocol behavior from design inference. Design Value: prevent keyword classifiers and permission fallbacks from becoming workflow policy. Constraints & Risks: protocol capabilities may change.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence connects repository behavior and official host protocols to the design.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Evidence.md contains repository evidence, primary sources, and bounded deductions.

goal: Map every task alignment requirement to focused and final proof.
description: Scope: define verification before completing implementation. Context: lifecycle context, privacy, configuration ownership, and Stop regression require distinct proof. Target: focused Hook and installer tests, one final suite, state validation, and a one-time obsolete-interface search. Design Considerations: test intended behavior without retaining obsolete interfaces as permanent fixtures. Design Value: ensure protocol claims and privacy guarantees are executable. Constraints & Risks: run the full suite only at final validation.

acceptance_criteria.text: Every requirement maps to focused and final proof.

commit.message: docs: map task Plan alignment validation

goal: Fix context, protocol, state, and delivery boundaries before completion.
description: Scope: define the lifecycle context architecture. Context: detection, task guidance, regression, and installation must not accumulate in one module. Target: separate structural scope, privacy-safe context, host translation, state enforcement, and managed configuration. Design Considerations: functional core, data minimization, capability fallback, and immutable history. Design Value: preserve one-way dependencies and independently testable responsibilities. Constraints & Risks: no semantic keyword matcher or tool permission gate.

acceptance_criteria.text: Architecture assigns one responsibility to each module and defines every lifecycle transition.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Architecture.md records module, lifecycle, privacy, and failure contracts.

goal: Inject structural session context and semantic task-to-Plan alignment guidance.
description: Scope: Closure: module - privacy-safe lifecycle context; scripts/hook_context.py, scripts/hook_tool.py, and tests/test_hook_tool.py. Context: the host agent must compare its latest task with canonical planning state without a script ingesting or classifying private prompt prose. Target: bounded Plan inventory, session standing rule, Codex/Claude prompt guidance, Cursor session fallback, and unchanged bounded Stop regression. Design Considerations: pure context builders, data-only inventory, and fail-open event dispatch. Design Value: semantic alignment uses the agent's full intent while deterministic code owns only structural facts. Constraints & Risks: never echo prompt or Plan prose and never deny tool execution.

acceptance_criteria.text: Session events inject a relative standing alignment rule only for one structural Better Plan workspace.
Codex and Claude prompt events request semantic latest-intent alignment and corrective planning; Cursor prompt fallback remains absent and nonblocking.
Private prompt text, Plan prose, concrete paths, and regression output are absent from Hook responses.

commit.message: feat: add task Plan lifecycle context

goal: Deliver the current host lifecycle mappings and operator contract.
description: Scope: Closure: capability - managed lifecycle delivery; scripts/hook_config.py, scripts/install.py, tests/test_install_tool.py, SKILL.md, and README.md. Context: context behavior is useful only when each host receives the exact nonblocking events it supports. Target: Codex/Claude session, prompt, and Stop handlers; Cursor session and Stop handlers; portable commands; idempotent doctor/uninstall; and clear Node-start semantics. Design Considerations: declarative event maps and ownership-marker replacement. Design Value: one current interface prevents old tool Hooks and unsupported prompt fallbacks from surviving. Constraints & Risks: preserve unrelated settings and leave local managed Hooks uninstalled.

acceptance_criteria.text: Managed configuration contains exactly the supported lifecycle events for each host and no tool-use event.
Install, update, doctor, and uninstall are portable, idempotent, and preserve unrelated handlers.
Skill and README explain session detection, semantic task alignment, internal Node start, Cursor capability limits, and Stop regression.

commit.message: feat: deliver task Plan lifecycle hooks
commit.target: Hook configuration, installer, tests, and documentation

goal: Prove task-to-Plan lifecycle alignment and preserve the requested local uninstall.
description: Scope: final validation of task alignment behavior, managed delivery, privacy, and state consistency. Context: each implementation module has focused proof. Target: run the full suite once, validate workspace structure and requirement labels, perform one obsolete-interface/privacy inventory, sync local skill code without Hooks, and confirm managed handler counts stay zero. Design Considerations: use only relative redacted evidence. Design Value: close the current Plan against the latest user intent. Constraints & Risks: do not reinstall local Hooks while synchronizing files.

acceptance_criteria.text: The complete unit suite passes once after all focused regressions.
Workspace validation and requirement-label checks pass.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Workspace structure, source resolution, and requirement labels validated with no warnings

acceptance_criteria.text: One-time inventory finds no tool-use Hook, obsolete interface, concrete path disclosure, or server/runtime data disclosure.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: One-time static inventory found no tool-use Hook, obsolete wrapper, concrete machine path, secret, server identity, or runtime-data artifact

acceptance_criteria.text: Local skill copies match the repository while managed Better Plan Hook counts remain zero.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Installed runtime files match the repository and managed Hook counts remain zero for all three Hook-capable hosts

commit.message: test: validate task Plan lifecycle alignment
